perturbation.original_text: You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than for internal review by your executive team.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'evaluating a Possible Transaction' to 'internal review by your executive team' broadens the permitted use of the Evaluation Materials. This conflicts with the overarching confidentiality and non-disclosure obligations elsewhere in the agreement, because it is not only reffering to the 'Possible Transaction'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to ----------------- those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you, and shall each expressly agree, to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement), (ii) you may disclose Evaluation Materials that you have been advised by counsel are required to be disclosed under applicable securities laws; and (iii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the use of Evaluation Materials to 'evaluating a Possible Transaction'. The changed text broadens the permitted use to 'internal review by your executive team'. This contradicts the Contradicted Text, which allows disclosure of Evaluation Materials only to those who need to know for the purpose of 'evaluating a Possible Transaction'. By allowing 'internal review by your executive team', the changed text permits use beyond the explicitly stated purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, conflicting with the confidentiality restrictions tied to that specific purpose in the contradicted section. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: You will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: You will only be responsible for a material breach of this Agreement by your representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The insertion of 'only be responsible for a material' softens the liability standard. It introduces a conflict with the previous, more strict standards, about the liability of a possible breach, thus contradicting the meaning and legal responsibility for the mentioned party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Other than with respect to those Evaluation Materials you have been advised by counsel are required to be disclosed under applicable securities laws, in the event that you or any of your representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, you or your representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that you will be responsible for any breach of the agreement by your representatives. The changed text limits this responsibility to only material breaches. The contradicted text requires representatives to take certain actions (notify, consult, assist) upon *any* request to disclose evaluation materials. By limiting the responsibility to material breaches only, the modified text contradicts the contradicted text, because the representatives no longer have the responsibility to notify, consult or assist in the event of a non-material breach, causing a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall use reasonable efforts to deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall promptly deliver' to 'shall use reasonable efforts to deliver' weakens the obligation to return or destroy Evaluation Materials promptly. This conflicts with the confidentiality obligations, because it is not being so strict about the returnment/destroy of the materials. Thus, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You recognize and acknowledge the confidential and non-public nature of the Evaluation Materials, the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials, and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates prompt delivery or destruction of evaluation materials. The changed text weakens this obligation by requiring only 'reasonable efforts' for delivery or destruction. This introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes the confidential nature of the materials and the potential damage from unauthorized use or disclosure. The weaker obligation in the changed text increases the risk of such unauthorized use or disclosure, creating a strong and clear inconsistency in the contract's approach to protecting confidential information, falling under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: You also agree that unless and until a formal written agreement between the Company and you with respect to a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered by you and each of the other parties thereto, there shall not be any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any such transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to such transaction except, in
perturbation.changed_text: You also agree that unless and until a preliminary agreement between the Company and you with respect to a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered by you and each of the other parties thereto, there shall not be any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any such transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to such transaction except, in
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'formal written agreement' to 'preliminary agreement' drastically alters the threshold for a legal obligation. This introduces a contradiction with the intention of not having legal obligation until a formal agreement is made. Now, a preliminary agreement it is enough to start a legal obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "formal written agreement" does not include an executed letter of intent or any other preliminary written agreement, nor does it include any oral acceptance of an offer or bid by you. You further understand and agree that (i) the Company shall be free to conduct the process for a Possible Transaction as it in its sole discretion shall determine (including, without limitation, negotiating with any prospective interested parties and entering into a formal agreement without prior notice to you or to any other person), (ii) any procedures relating to such Possible Transaction may be changed at any time without notice to you or any other person and (iii) unless and until a formal written agreement concerning the Possible Transaction has been executed, neither the Company or any of its directors, officers, employees, stockholders, owners, affiliates, agents or representatives will have any liability to you with respect to the Possible Transaction, whether by virtue of this letter agreement, any other written or oral expression regarding a Possible Transaction or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that a 'formal written agreement' was required to establish a legal obligation, aligning with the contradicted text's definition of 'formal written agreement' explicitly excluding preliminary agreements. The changed text replaces 'formal written agreement' with 'preliminary agreement', directly contradicting the contradicted text's intent to avoid legal obligations based on preliminary agreements. This creates a strong in-text contradiction by lowering the threshold for legal obligation and rendering the definition of 'formal written agreement' in the contradicted text irrelevant and contradictory. As per the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction', key obligations are defined inconsistently.
